title: Xtra Smoky British Lamb Kebabs
description: A smoky and flavorful dish featuring lamb, peach juice, and a Mediterranean-inspired glaze, perfect for adventurous eaters looking for a taste of Northern Irish cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, aiming for an internal temperature of 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together peach juice, olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade, covering the bowl with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate the lamb for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to penetrate.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the kebabs are cooking, prepare the glaze by whisking together honey and lemon juice in a small bowl.
Brush the glaze onto the kebabs during the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Remove the kebabs from the grill and sprinkle with chopped rosemary.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with additional rosemary if desired.
